title: Proclamation Recognizing Peace Officers Memorial Day and Police Week 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation issued to commemorate Peace Officers Memorial Day and Police Week in 2026. The proclamation acknowledges law enforcement officers who have died in the line of duty and recognizes the contributions of active law enforcement personnel to public safety and community protection. The proclamation is part of the annual observance during Police Week to honor deceased officers and celebrate the service of law enforcement across the United States.

title: Presidential Nominations Submitted to Senate for Cabinet and Agency Positions
summary: The President submitted multiple nominations to the Senate for various executive branch positions including Deputy Secretary of the Treasury, Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency within the Department of Homeland Security, and Assistant Secretary of State for European and Eurasian Affairs, among other positions. The submission also included at least one withdrawal of a previous nomination.

title: Proclamation Designating May 2026 as Military Spouse Day
summary: This presidential proclamation designates a specific day in May 2026 to recognize and honor military spouses. The proclamation acknowledges the contributions and sacrifices of military families throughout American history and their role in supporting the Armed Forces. It recognizes that military spouses have provided essential support to service members across generations since the nation's founding.

title: Presidential Proclamation Commemorating Victory in Europe Day 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation establishing a commemoration of Victory in Europe Day on May 8th, 2026, marking the anniversary of Germany's surrender on May 8, 1945, and the conclusion of World War II in Europe. The proclamation acknowledges the military contributions of the United States Armed Forces and Allied nations in the European theater during World War II.

title: Proclamation on National Physical Fitness and Sports Month, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation designating May 2026 as National Physical Fitness and Sports Month. The proclamation recognizes the value of physical fitness and sports participation in American society, acknowledging the discipline and competitive elements inherent in athletic pursuits. The proclamation affirms commitment to promoting healthy lifestyles among the population and expanding access to fitness and sports opportunities.

title: Proclamation Establishing Jewish American Heritage Month
summary: A presidential proclamation designating a month to recognize and honor the historical contributions of Jewish Americans to the United States. The proclamation acknowledges Jewish American participation in the nation's development over its 250 years of independence and references values including faith, family, and freedom. This type of proclamation typically calls for public recognition and observance of the designated heritage month through appropriate ceremonies and programs.

title: Presidential Permit for Bridger Pipeline Expansion LLC to Construct and Operate Pipeline Facilities at U.S.-Canada Border in Montana
summary: Presidential permit granted to Bridger Pipeline Expansion LLC, a privately held limited liability company, authorizing the construction, connection, operation, and maintenance of pipeline facilities at the international boundary between the United States and Canada in Phillips County, Montana. The permit is subject to conditions specified within the authorization document.

title: Proclamation Designating National Fallen Firefighters Memorial Weekend 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation establishing a national observance to commemorate volunteer and career firefighters who have died in the line of duty. The proclamation honors firefighters who have made fatal sacrifices during their service, with particular recognition for those lost during the preceding year. The observance provides an official designation for a weekend of remembrance and recognition of firefighter casualties.

title: Proclamation Designating April 2026 as National Foster Care Month
summary: A presidential proclamation that officially designates April 2026 as National Foster Care Month. The proclamation acknowledges that hundreds of thousands of American children are served by the foster care system and calls for national recognition of foster care during this designated month. The proclamation invites Americans to participate in recognizing and supporting foster care efforts, children in foster care, and those who work within the foster care system.
